markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Bands Of Imprisonment](/Ikons.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 43

**Usage** worn anklets, bracers, or circlet (often a headband)

</column>

---

These weighted bands don’t enhance your power—rather, they keep your strength in check, honing your discipline.

**Immanence** The _bands of imprisonment tighten_, keeping your mind sharp. You gain a +1 status bonus to Will saving throws and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage equal to half your level.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Break Free;     **Effect** You can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) with a +2 status bonus on your check, then [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to twice your Speed in a straight line, and finally make a melee Strike. If you don’t need to Escape or you can’t move or choose not to, you still take the other actions listed.
name: Bands Of Imprisonment

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Mortal Harvest](/Ikons.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 45

**Usage** a [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364) or any weapon from the [axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1), [flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7), or [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11) group

</column>

---

This weapon, once used for felling trees or crops, now harvests lives instead.

**Immanence** The mortal harvest deals 1 [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) per weapon damage die to creatures it [Strikes](/Rules.aspx?ID=2895).

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Reap the Field;    **Requirements** Your previous action was a successful Strike with the _mortal harvest_; **Effect** Time seems to lag as you blur across the battlefield, deciding the fate of many in a moment. [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to half your Speed and make another melee Strike with the mortal harvest against a different creature. This Strike uses the same multiple attack penalty as your previous Strike, but counts toward your multiple attack penalty as normal.
name: Mortal Harvest

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Noble Branch](/Ikons.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 45

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The phrase “damage equal to your weapon's damage dice.” is used. This is not the number of damage dice, but refers to rolling the weapon's damage dice._

**Usage** a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367), [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372), [fighting stick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=168), [khakkara](/Weapons.aspx?ID=412), or any weapon in the [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14) or [polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11) weapon group

</column>

---

This humble stick-like weapon has an elegant simplicity to it, affording you reliable strikes over flashy maneuvers.

**Immanence** The _noble branch_ deals 2 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage per weapon damage die to creatures it Strikes.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Strike, Breathe, Rend;    **Requirements** Your last action this turn was a successful Strike with the _noble branch_; **Effect** You channel a rending pulse of energy down your weapon in the moment of contact. The target of the Strike takes spirit damage equal to the _noble branch’s_ weapon damage dice. This includes any extra dice from [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) runes, but not from special abilities, [property runes](/Rules.aspx?ID=3168), or the like.
name: Noble Branch

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Pelt Of The Beast](/Ikons.aspx?ID=12)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 45

**Usage** worn cloak or belt

</column>

---

This animal hide, whether worn about the shoulders or waist, is all you need to survive even the harshest elements. When you make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), choose [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage. The pelt attunes to that damage type.

**Immanence** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) equal to half your level to the damage type the pelt is attuned to. You also treat [environmental cold and heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) as one step less severe.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Survive the Wilds;     **Effect** You wrap the pelt around yourself. You can choose to change the damage type the pelt is attuned to. The pelt shines gold, drawing the offending energies into itself. Until the start of your next turn, this shine creates an aura in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). You and all allies in the emanation gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against effects with that trait.
name: Pelt Of The Beast

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Body Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Scar Of The Survivor](/Ikons.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 45

**Usage** imbued in the skin

</column>

---

A scar on your body commemorates a time someone tried to end your story and failed—a testament to your resilience and fortitude.

**Immanence** Divine energy spreads outward from your scar, reinforcing your flesh. You gain the benefits of the [Diehard](/Feats.aspx?ID=5140) feat and a +1 status bonus to Fortitude saving throws.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842), [Vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509))No Scar but This;     **Effect** Your wounds knit shut with hardly a scratch. You regain 1d8 Hit Points. At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, the healing increases by 1d8.
name: Scar Of The Survivor

</summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Shadow Sheath](/Ikons.aspx?ID=14)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 45

**Usage** a holster or sheath shaped for a one-handed [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=711) weapon of light Bulk or less

</column>

---

With an infinite array of [darts](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=6), [throwing knives](/Weapons.aspx?ID=243), or similar weapons, you never need worry about being unarmed. You can spend 1 minute to load a weapon meeting the _shadow sheath’s_ usage requirements into the sheath, which is hidden somewhere on your person. As long as the _shadow sheath_ is on your person, you can [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to draw an exact copy of the weapon from thin air. These copies retain the runes and abilities of the hidden weapon, though if you use any limited-use abilities (such as [talismans](/Traits.aspx?ID=709) or Activations with a frequency limit), they count against the weapon’s normal usages. A copy disappears shortly after leaving your hand (or being used for a thrown [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306)).

**Immanence** You can Interact to draw a weapon from the shadow sheath as a free action. Your Strikes with a weapon produced from the shadow sheath deal 2 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage per weapon damage die, which increases to 3 per die if the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58).

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Liar’s Hidden Blade;    **Requirements** Your previous action was an unsuccessful Strike with the weapon from the _shadow sheath_; **Effect** The shadow weapon you threw fades, the distraction covering your true intention all along—a second strike in hidden in the blind spot of the first! Interact to draw another weapon from the shadow sheath, then Strike with it at the same multiple attack penalty as the unsuccessful attack. The opponent is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to this attack. This Strike counts toward your multiple attack penalty as normal. After the Strike resolves, you can Interact to draw another weapon from the _shadow sheath_.
name: Shadow Sheath

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Body Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Skin Hard As Horn](/Ikons.aspx?ID=15)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 46

**Usage** imbued in the skin

</column>

---

Tempered in your spirit, your very skin is as a suit of armor, though a single location on your body remains unprotected, a curse and a challenge within your legend. During your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), you can strike your skin lightly with an object that deals bludgeoning, slashing or piecing damage to habituate your skin against this type of injury, attuning the ikon to that damage type.

**Immanence** When your skin houses your divine spark, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to the attuned damage type equal to half your level. This resistance doesn’t apply against critical hits, which successfully find your unprotected spot.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Crash against Me;     **Effect** Your skin becomes virtually unbreakable. Until the start of your next turn, you have resistance equal to your level to the chosen damage type. During this time, if a creature attacking you using a weapon dealing the same damage type as your resistance misses you or hits you but deals no damage due to your resistance, the weapon clangs wildly off your skin. This painful reverberation makes the attacking enemy [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and gives it a –2 circumstance penalty to attacks with that weapon until the start of the enemy’s next turn.
name: Skin Hard As Horn
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 46

**Usage** imbued in the skin
</additional-info>

---

</summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Skybearer's Belt](/Ikons.aspx?ID=16)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 46

**Usage** worn belt

</column>

---

This girdle wraps around your waist, magnifying your strength to the point you feel you could carry the sky itself.

**Immanence** Strength flows forth. You can attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) creatures up to two sizes larger than you, and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks for these actions and to your saving throws to resist these actions.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Bear Allies’ Burdens;     **Effect** You move with a speed belying your strength, carrying your allies as easily as straw dolls. You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). At any point you are adjacent to a willing ally during the Stride, you can pick that ally up, and you can deposit them into a space adjacent to you at any other point during your movement. You ignore the ally’s Bulk while carrying them during your Stride. You can [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type.
name: Skybearer's Belt

---

</summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Starshot](/Ikons.aspx?ID=17)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 46

**Usage** a ranged weapon

</column>

---

You might be the only one capable of stringing this bow or pulling this trigger; either way, the ikon’s shots are packed with explosive power, striking like falling stars.

**Immanence** Strikes with the _starshot_ deal an additional 1 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage per weapon damage die.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Giant-Felling Comet;     **Effect** You shoot the _starshot_, causing a detonation in a 5-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet. Each creature in the area must succeed at a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC or take spirit damage equal to your normal Strike damage with the starshot. Creatures larger than you take a –2 circumstance penalty to their saving throws. This shot requires any ammunition that would normally be required for the weapon.
name: Starshot

<additional-info>
**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 46

**Usage** a ranged weapon
</additional-info>

---

You might be the only one capable of stringing this bow or pulling this trigger; either way, the ikon’s shots are packed with explosive power, …
</summary>

Creatures larger than you take a –2 circumstance penalty to their saving throws. This shot requires any ammunition that would normally be required for the weapon.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Thousand-League Sandals](/Ikons.aspx?ID=18)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 46

**Usage** worn shoes

</column>

---

Threadbare but trustworthy, your sandals have carried you this far, and they’ll carry you much further still.

**Immanence** Your sandals ease your travels on the path ahead, granting you a +10-foot status bonus to your [Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2894).

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Marathon Dash;     **Effect** Your feet carry you so quickly they leave a slipstream that speeds your allies on. You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). Each ally within 10 feet of you at the start of your movement can Stride as a reaction.
name: Thousand-League Sandals

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Titan's Breaker](/Ikons.aspx?ID=19)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 46

**Usage** any melee weapon in the [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5), [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8), or [axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) group, or any your melee [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) [Strikes](/Rules.aspx?ID=2895) that deals bludgeoning damage

</column>

---

You wield a weapon whose blows shatter mountains with ease.

**Immanence** The _titan’s breaker_ deals 2 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage per weapon damage die to creatures it Strikes. [Constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=563) and [objects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2363) are not immune to this spirit damage, and this spirit damage automatically bypasses an amount of their Hardness equal to your level.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Fracture Mountains;     **Effect** Your spirit is so dense it takes on tangible force. Make a melee Strike with the _titan’s breaker_. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. If this Strike hits, your additional spirit damage from the ikon’s immanence increases to 4 plus an extra die of weapon damage. If you’re at least 10th level, it’s increased to 6 spirit damage and two extra dice, and if you’re at least 18th level, it’s increased to 8 spirit damage and three extra dice.
name: Titan's Breaker

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Barrow's Edge](/Ikons.aspx?ID=2)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 43

**Usage** melee weapon that deals slashing or piercing damage

</column>

---

This blade subtly rattles in its scabbard, as if it wants to be unsheathed so it can consume violence.

**Immanence** The _barrow’s edge_ deals 1 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage per weapon damage die to a creature it Strikes. If the creature is below half its maximum Hit Points, the weapon deals 3 additional spirit damage per weapon damage dice instead. _Barrow’s edge_ rattles more strongly when an enemy below half its maximum Hit Points is within the weapon’s reach to let you know a foe is verging closer to death.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842), [Vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509))Drink of my Foes;    **Requirements** Your last action was a successful Strike with the _barrow’s edge_; **Effect** Your blade glows as it absorbs your foe’s vitality. You regain Hit Points equal to half the damage dealt.
name: Barrow's Edge

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Unfailing Bow](/Ikons.aspx?ID=20)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 47

**Usage** a ranged weapon

</column>

---

The shots fired by this weapon seem guided by divine accuracy, finding the swiftest targets.

**Immanence** The _unfailing bow_ deals 1 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage per weapon damage die to creatures it [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), or 1d4 additional spirit damage per weapon die on a critical hit.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Arrow Splits Arrow;    **Requirements** Your previous action was to Strike with the _unfailing bow_; **Effect** You repeat your motions exactly, your attack landing in the same location as your previous shot. You make a Strike against the same target. The result of your d20 roll is the same as the result of the required shot, though any penalties (such as your multiple attack penalty) apply normally to this shot and you don’t automatically adjust the degree of success if the initial roll was a natural 1 or 20.
name: Unfailing Bow

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Victor's Wreath](/Ikons.aspx?ID=21)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 47

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The Transcendence effect of _Victor’s Wreath_ allows a new saving throw, so anything that happens on a failed save still occurs if a PC fails. It cannot target curses._

**Usage** worn headwear or belt (typically a sash)

</column>

---

This symbol of victory—whether a laurel worn around the head or a medal that hangs from your neck—reminds you and your allies that victory is the only acceptable outcome.

**Immanence** You inspire your allies to greater glory. You and all your allies in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))One Moment till Glory;     **Effect** You rally your allies, carrying them from the brink of disaster to the verge of victory. Each ally in your aura can immediately attempt a new saving throw with a +2 status bonus against one ongoing negative effect or condition currently affecting it that required a save. Use the result of the new save to determine the outcome of the effect, unless the new save would have a worse result than the original save, in which case nothing happens. Each ally can gain this benefit against a given effect only once.
name: Victor's Wreath

<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Fetching Bangles](/Ikons.aspx?ID=3)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 44

**Usage** worn bracers

</column>

---

These lovely armbands sparkle and gleam, reflecting your own incredible magnetism.

**Immanence** Others find it hard to leave your captivating presence. An aura surrounds you in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). An enemy in the aura that attempts to move away from you must succeed at a Will save against your class DC or its [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action is disrupted.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Embrace of Destiny;     **Effect** Choose an enemy within 20 feet of you. It must succeed at a Will save against your class DC or be pulled directly toward you into a square adjacent to you.
name: Fetching Bangles

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Body Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Eye-Catching Spot](/Ikons.aspx?ID=4)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 44

**Usage** imbued on the face

</column>

---

A fetching beauty spot under an eye or a smile as warm as the sun distracts foes and captures hearts alike.

**Immanence** Your beauty becomes supernaturally enhanced, distracting foes and imposing a –1 circumstance penalty to melee attack rolls against you.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727))Captivating Charm;     **Effect** You focus your attention on a creature within 30 feet, overwhelming its senses. The creature must succeed at a Will save against your class DC or be [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by you until the start of your next turn. The condition ends if you use a [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) action against the target, but not if you use a hostile action against its allies.
name: Eye-Catching Spot

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Body Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Gaze Sharp As Steel](/Ikons.aspx?ID=5)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 44

**Usage** imbued in the eyes

</column>

---

Your eyes glint with an almost-tangible sharpness, letting you spot the tiniest swallow on the horizon or the swiftest arrow in flight.

**Immanence** Your vision sharpens and allows you to sense an enemy’s attack almost as soon as it begins, granting you a +1 status bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks and a +2 status bonus to your AC against ranged attacks.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))A Moment Unending;     **Effect** You take in every movement around you, affording you unparalleled accuracy. Your next successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against an enemy before the end of your next turn deals an additional 1d6 precision damage. This damage increases to 2d6 at 10th level and 3d6 at 18th level.
name: Gaze Sharp As Steel

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Gleaming Blade](/Ikons.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 44

**Usage** a weapon in the [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15) or [knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9) group, or a melee unarmed attack that deals slashing damage

</column>

---

This blade glitters with such sharpness it seems to cut the very air in front of it.

**Immanence** Strikes with the _gleaming blade_ deal 2 additional [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage per weapon damage die.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Flowing Spirit Strike;     **Effect** Make two Strikes with the gleaming blade, each against the same target and using your current multiple attack penalty. If the gleaming blade doesn’t have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) trait, the second Strike takes a –2 penalty. If both attacks hit, you combine their damage, which is all dealt as spirit damage. You add any precision damage only once. Combine the damage from both Strikes and apply [resistances and weaknesses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) only once. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty.
name: Gleaming Blade

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Weapon Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Hands Of The Wildling](/Ikons.aspx?ID=7)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 44

**Usage** a melee [free-hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613) weapon or a melee [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) Strike

</column>

---

Tattooed fists, savage claws, or even powerful gauntlets—you swing each with the fury of an animal from the woods.

**Immanence** [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with your hands of the wildling deal an additional 1 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage per weapon damage die. You are [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to this splash damage.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Feral Swing;     **Effect** You lash out with both arms, rending all before you. Each creature in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) must succeed at a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save against your class DC or take spirit damage equal to your normal Strike damage with your hands of the wildling. You can choose to swing with abandon, which imposes a –2 circumstance bonus to enemies’ saving throws, but causes you to become [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.
name: Hands Of The Wildling

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Horn Of Plenty](/Ikons.aspx?ID=8)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 44

**Usage** a wallet, bag, or similar container of 1 Bulk you can wear on your body

</column>

---

Whether a bag, gourd, wallet, cornucopia, or similar food receptacle, this ikon recalls the harvest and hearth. The receptacle can store up to 1 Bulk of potions and elixirs, but no other items. The receptacle can’t be opened except by the ikon’s immanence and transcendence abilities.

Each day during your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575), the ikon produces one temporary [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308). You can choose to have it make a different [elixir](/Traits.aspx?ID=589) or [potion](/Traits.aspx?ID=672) you know the formula for. The level of any elixir or potion created by the horn must be your level or lower. The number of elixirs the horn creates increases to two at 8th level and three at 16th level, and you can choose each item individually. These temporary items vanish the next time you make your daily preparations, and any remaining effects of the temporary items end. A temporary elixir or potion has no value.

**Immanence** The _horn of plenty_ shimmers, allowing access to the stored consumables inside. You can [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to draw a consumable and drink it in a single action while your divine spark rests within the horn. Other creatures can’t access the contents unless you allow them to.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Feed the Masses;     **Effect** The _horn of plenty_ allows you to transfer the effects of potions and elixirs to your allies. You Interact to draw a consumable from the horn and then Interact to drink it. Rather than nourishing yourself, the item’s effects are transferred to a willing ally within 60 feet, as if they had consumed it themself. If the consumable restores Hit Points, you can choose to divide the amount it restores between you and the ally freely (after rolling dice to determine the amount).
name: Horn Of Plenty

text: Horn Of Plenty Source War of Immortals pg. 44 Usage a wallet, bag, or similar container of 1 Bulk you can wear on your body --- Whether a bag, gourd, wallet, cornucopia, or similar food receptacle, this ikon recalls the harvest and hearth. The receptacle can store up to 1 Bulk of potions and elixirs, but no other items. The receptacle can’t be opened except by the ikon’s immanence and transcendence abilities. Each day during your daily preparations, the ikon produces one temporary elixir of life. You can choose to have it make a different elixir or potion you know the formula for. The level of any elixir or potion created by the horn must be your level or lower. The number of elixirs the horn creates increases to two at 8th level and three at 16th level, and you can choose each item individually. These temporary items vanish the next time you make your daily preparations, and any remaining effects of the temporary items end. A temporary elixir or potion has no value. Immanence The horn of plenty shimmers, allowing access to the stored consumables inside. You can Interact to draw a consumable and drink it in a single action while your divine spark rests within the horn. Other creatures can’t access the contents unless you allow them to. Transcendence Single Action (Transcendence)Feed the Masses; Effect The horn of plenty allows you to transfer the effects of potions and elixirs to your allies. You Interact to draw a consumable from the horn and then Interact to drink it. Rather than nourishing yourself, the item’s effects are transferred to a willing ally within 60 feet, as if they had consumed it themself. If the consumable restores Hit Points, you can choose to divide the amount it restores between you and the ally freely (after rolling dice to determine the amount).
usage: a wallet, bag, or similar container of 1 Bulk you can wear on your body

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Exemplar Worn Ikon " pfs="Standard">[Mirrored Aegis](/Ikons.aspx?ID=9)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 45

**Usage** any [shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12)

</column>

---

This shield is polished so brightly it can reflect even [spiritual](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) and [ethereal](/Traits.aspx?ID=593) attacks.

**Immanence** The _mirrored aegis_ emits an aura in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that protects you and all allies in the aura from harm, granting a +1 status bonus to AC. If the _mirrored aegis_ houses your divine spark for 10 uninterrupted minutes, it is restored to full Hit Points.

**Transcendence** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [Transcendence](/Traits.aspx?ID=842))Raise the Walls;     **Effect** You [Raise](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316) the _mirrored aegis_, which summons ethereal shields that surround you and one ally of your choice within 15 feet in a tortoise shield formation. You and the ally gain a +1 status bonus to AC, Reflex saves, and any save against a force, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) effect for 1 minute.
name: Mirrored Aegis
